mohnuin.text: Really cool. Maybe as an upsell, you could allow users to "gift a response" as part of their letter. In other words, prepaying for their recipient's response. The letter could come with a tiny tag or something where the recipient would essentially be able to scan a QR code or go to a custom link that lets them send a letter back to the original sender at no cost to them. Added benefit of increasing user adoption.

mohn7wu.text: Just some thoughts to help the flow. - !!! Nice subtle gradient animation in the background. - The header logo can be clicked on within the main-page and I get a little nod and a wink, but visiting other pages like your About, Privacy, Terms does not show the animation and does provide a link back to the Main Page. So you might add a "Call to Action" / 'Send a letter' button in a top corner of your header so visitors are always brought back to the main page. - Lastly, I think the navigation on the bottom of your site could be placed above your page header below your little hat icon. Either Darken the text so it has more contrast over the background or enlarge the font size a bit more. It's just a bit too difficult to see the navigation and you have to scroll down to see it when you first visit your domain. - EDIT: Ideas just keep popping up. I think people would get a kick out of the carbon footprint that is reduced by utilizing this service. If I sent a piece of mail from Boise, Idaho to London, England, I'd be contributing to a fair amount of pollution to get that piece from point A to point B. If I was to use your service, it would be cool to see how the letters carbon footprint has been reduced. What would be the GHG comparison between the two deliveries. ----- Could I pay more to expedite a delivery or add a dozen roses to it with a box of chocolates? I love this service!

n90iqpt.text: Aw man. Well I'd demand proof of it being sent out or else a refund, their costumer service was really sketchy and the no tracking is also pretty weird :/ even though initially on their website it said there's international tracking, when I contacted their support about it they said it was "lost in translation". The person who built this website has built another site just for the money, a "pay to message" site. Starting to think that's how they profit, by making people believe the "cheap 3£ letters" are worth it since some of us can't send it out and this service does it for you...and then boom, collects 3£ from thousands of people, easy money. Idk what I expected but I'm not surprised, this is the third letter service I've seen scamming. The pictures look fake too.

n90khsd.text: yeah very fishy, only two options credit card and...ideal? never heard of that 😭 unfortunately I was dumb enough to trust this site because their original post blew up, no idea what they're going to do with that address and credit card information now

mohecln.text: Very nice idea, and good execution! mok1h5j.text: I do not know if it’s country specific thing but in Bulgaria when you send letters, the sender names and addresses are in top left corner and the recipient is in the bottom right, stamp is on top right of the envelope. Additionally the name of the sender and address is put in the first sheet of the letter itself again in the top left corner. Nevertheless, maybe not necessarily needed but it’s adding personal touch to it. One more suggestion, you can experiment with handwriting fonts customisations, will be not the same as the personal handwriting but still is getting close to the overall idea of the letters :) Map page is a bit hard to navigate (personally) and to check which countries are filled. Maybe will be good addition to add country list and count of sent letters (something like leaderboard) Good luck with it!

molj9e1.text: The address doesn't adapt to regional address standards. While in Belgium and the Netherlands, the building number follows the street, in North America, the custom is that the number precedes the street in the first line of the address. This might cause some small confusion and would help if remedied.

molsisf.text: That's a really nice idea. I would love to have a feature where I can schedule a delivery date for the mail. For ex - a birthday greeting to be delivered on a particular day. Your website is catchy and it feels smooth.

Just a heads up, since this is also used for the famous children cartoon (Dutch version of Postman Pat):))

mpm25tx.text: How are you preventing hate, terrorism related or similar things ? They could just send through post and the sender will be you via post.

mrg3fq4.text: Had a similar idea. But the issue was that our country takes terrorism related things very seriously and it is a non-bailable offense here. If someone sends a message to those people through us, we will obviously be the sender and could be linked. This could be like an undetected mode of communication. But you could end up being a part of it unknowingly.

mrfuk50.text: this is my question too! i didn't see anything in the terms and conditions but hopefully OP can still update it

mrfu1al.text: hi OP, i really like your project so far but i have some suggestions: - allow other payment methods such as paypal. some people are very conscious about sharing their card details and are not willing to easily share it, but are willing to use paypal or something else (update: just saw that you also accept apple pay as well. sorry, i didn't see that when i was typing this comment. but adding paypal would be really neat as well) - are you going to review the content of the letters? as someone else in this thread said, this might be used to harass other people so there should be some safeguards regarding abuse and harassment - i haven't tried sending a letter via your service yet but i hope there are other choices for the stamp (this is completely optional though and your default stamp is perfectly fine) - an app would be neat as well! especially for the ipad (i could totally understand if you don't have the resources for this, this is just a suggestion. your site is really neat already so this isn't necessary) - while i don't mind the font that you use, it would be neat if there are choices for font styles as well. i feel other typewritten or monospaced fonts would still fit your aesthetic while providing some variety. (i'm personally not into sans serif or handwritten fonts and i don't think they fit your aesthetic but hey, if you want to include them that's also fine) - i wish there was an option to add more files or to resize the images, so that we can layout the letter to avoid wasting space and paper that's all i have for now. will update this comment if i had other ideas!
